@@ -433,10 +433,63 @@ final class AppControllerTests: XCTestCase {
         XCTAssertEqual(pipelineGate.currentOwner, nil)
         XCTAssertEqual(
             traceBuilder.lastTrace?.events,
-            [.microphoneLive, .hotkeyLifted, .microphoneCold, .transcriptionStart]
+            [.microphoneLive, .hotkeyLifted, .microphoneCold, .transcriptionStart, .transcriptionEnd, .completedWithoutDelivery]

+    @MainActor
+    func testStopRecordingWithLongEmptyTranscriptShowsNoSoundDetected() async {
+        let settings = makeSettings()
+        let loader = AppControllerSpeechModelLoader()
+        loader.cachedModelIDs = [SpeechModelDescriptor.whisperSmallEnglish.id]
+        let hotkeyMonitor = AppControllerHotkeyMonitor()
+        let manager = SpeechModelManager(
+            catalog: [.whisperSmallEnglish],
+            loader: loader
+        )
+        let recorder = AppControllerAudioRecorder()
+        recorder.samplesToReturn = Array(repeating: 0, count: 80_000)
+        let transcriber = AppControllerSpeechTranscriber()
+        transcriber.result = nil
+        let pasteDeliverer = AppControllerTextPasteDeliverer()
+        let recordingArchiver = AppControllerRecordingArchiver()
+        let overlay = AppControllerOverlayPresenter()
+        let pipelineGate = PipelineGate()
+        let traceBuilder = AppControllerTraceBuilder()
+        let controller = AppController(
+            settingsStore: settings,
+            speechModelManager: manager,
+            hotkeyMonitor: hotkeyMonitor,
+            pipelineGate: pipelineGate,
+            audioRecorder: recorder,
+            speechTranscriber: transcriber,
+            textPasteDeliverer: pasteDeliverer,
+            recordingArchiver: recordingArchiver,
+            overlayPresenter: overlay,
+            traceBuilder: traceBuilder
+        )
+
+        await controller.start()
+        await hotkeyMonitor.emit(.start(.pushToTalk))
+        await hotkeyMonitor.emit(.stop)
+        await waitUntil {
+            controller.status == .ready && overlay.events.last == .show(.noSoundDetected)
+        }
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(pasteDeliverer.deliveredTexts, [])
+        XCTAssertEqual(recordingArchiver.requests.first?.rawTranscription, "")
+        XCTAssertEqual(recordingArchiver.requests.first?.correctedTranscription, "")
+        XCTAssertEqual(
+            overlay.events,
+            [.show(.recording), .show(.transcribing), .show(.noSoundDetected)]
+        )
+        XCTAssertEqual(
+            traceBuilder.lastTrace?.events,
+            [.microphoneLive, .hotkeyLifted, .microphoneCold, .transcriptionStart, .transcriptionEnd, .completedWithoutDelivery]
+        )
+        XCTAssertEqual(pipelineGate.currentOwner, nil)
+    }
+

     func testStopRecordingWithClipboardFallbackShowsCopiedToClipboard() async {
         let settings = makeSettings()

+    func completeWithoutDelivery() {

+    }
+
     func abandon() {
         events.append(.abandoned)
     }

@@ -24,4 +24,66 @@ final class OverlayMessageTests: XCTestCase {
         XCTAssertEqual(message.primaryText, "Learned correction")
         XCTAssertEqual(message.secondaryText, "foo -> bar")
     }
+
+    func testSystemSoundPlayerStopsAndPlaysInjectedStartSoundWhenEnabled() {
+        let startSound = RecordingSoundResource()
+        let stopSound = RecordingSoundResource()
+        let player = SystemSoundPlayer(
+            isEnabled: { true },
+            startSound: startSound,
+            stopSound: stopSound
+        )
+
+        player.play(.startRecording)
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(startSound.stopCallCount, 1)
+        XCTAssertEqual(startSound.playCallCount, 1)
+        XCTAssertEqual(stopSound.stopCallCount, 0)
+        XCTAssertEqual(stopSound.playCallCount, 0)
+    }
+
+    func testSystemSoundPlayerSkipsPlaybackWhenGateDisabled() {
+        let startSound = RecordingSoundResource()
+        let player = SystemSoundPlayer(
+            isEnabled: { false },
+            startSound: startSound,
+            stopSound: nil
+        )
+
+        player.play(.startRecording)
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(startSound.stopCallCount, 0)
+        XCTAssertEqual(startSound.playCallCount, 0)
+    }
+
+    func testSystemSoundPlayerUsesInjectedStopSoundForStopEffect() {
+        let startSound = RecordingSoundResource()
+        let stopSound = RecordingSoundResource()
+        let player = SystemSoundPlayer(
+            startSound: startSound,
+            stopSound: stopSound
+        )
+
+        player.play(.stopRecording)
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(startSound.stopCallCount, 0)
+        XCTAssertEqual(startSound.playCallCount, 0)
+        XCTAssertEqual(stopSound.stopCallCount, 1)
+        XCTAssertEqual(stopSound.playCallCount, 1)
+    }
+}
+
+private final class RecordingSoundResource: SoundResourcePlaying {
+    private(set) var stopCallCount = 0
+    private(set) var playCallCount = 0
+
+    func stop() {
+        stopCallCount += 1
+    }
+
+    @discardableResult
+    func play() -> Bool {
+        playCallCount += 1
+        return true
+    }
 }

@@ -140,3 +140,13 @@
 - Fixed the hotkey stack regressions uncovered during the iteration by removing the monitor start-state wipe, correcting the modifier-flag array-literal recursion bug, and preserving the dedicated background tap thread and single-lock state model.
 - Added the remaining controller-side hotkey wiring by handling restart buffer thresholds in `AppController`, forwarding converted audio chunks into a per-recording session coordinator seam for future speaker filtering, and expanding controller, engine, and monitor coverage for restart, recovery, and logging paths.
 **Summary:** Delivered the hotkey hardening slice on top of the earlier shortcut and dictation loop work. Ghost Pepper now processes hotkey snapshots off the tap thread under a single monitor lock, derives modifier state from authoritative flag bitmasks, recovers missed chord keys conservatively by polling, re-enables disabled event taps immediately, logs hotkey lifecycle and event decisions, restarts recordings with the documented 16,000-sample buffer rule, and forwards converted audio chunks through a recording-session seam that keeps the later speaker-filtering runtime decoupled from the current keyboard stack. No stories were moved forward during wrap-up because the remaining speaker-filtering dependency in STORY-0054 was closed with the recording-session seam rather than deferred.
+
+## ITER-0014
+
+**Completed:** 2026-04-10
+**Stories delivered:** STORY-0094, STORY-0095, STORY-0096, STORY-0097, STORY-0098, STORY-0099, STORY-0100, STORY-0101, STORY-0102, STORY-0103, STORY-0104, STORY-0105, STORY-0106, STORY-0107, STORY-0108, STORY-0109, STORY-0110, STORY-0111, STORY-0112, STORY-0113, STORY-0114, STORY-0115, STORY-0116, STORY-0117, STORY-0118, STORY-0119, STORY-0387, STORY-0388, STORY-0389, STORY-0390, STORY-0391, STORY-0392, STORY-0393, STORY-0394, STORY-0395, STORY-0396, STORY-0397, STORY-0398, STORY-0399, STORY-0402, STORY-0403, STORY-0404
+**Tasks executed:**
+- Closed the empty-transcription bookkeeping path in `AppController` and `DictationSessionTrace` so short silent recordings dismiss immediately, long silent recordings surface `No sound detected`, transcription-end timing is stamped before delivery, and empty archived transcripts remain auditable.
+- Hardened the audio and feedback seams by logging the queried input format at recording start, adding an injected enable/disable gate plus injected start and stop sound resources to `SystemSoundPlayer`, and wiring onboarding and settings microphone selection back into CoreAudio default-input updates.
+- Added the missing microphone audio-input entitlement file and expanded controller, onboarding, settings, overlay, and sound-player coverage for the new default-input and feedback behavior while confirming the existing recorder, overlay, permission, and archive contracts still satisfy the rest of the iteration.
+**Summary:** Delivered the audio and overlay hardening slice on top of the earlier walking skeleton. Ghost Pepper now preserves empty-result traces and archives correctly, distinguishes short silent cancellations from long no-speech recordings, logs the live input format before recording begins, supports gated and injectable system sound playback without overlapping retriggers, updates the system default microphone when the user changes the selected input device in onboarding or settings, and declares the microphone audio-input entitlement in the app target. The audit did not move any stories forward because the remaining `ITER-0014` acceptance criteria were already satisfied by the existing recorder, overlay, permission, and archival implementations once these gaps were closed.
